name: Mastodon Publisher
tagline: Posts news.article summaries to a Mastodon instance using the Mastodon API.
description:
A consumer worker that reads `news.article` items from the BFrost Item Bus and posts each one as a toot to a configured Mastodon instance. Supports custom post templates, hashtag injection from article tags, and a 500-character truncation guard with a link appended.

Authenticate via an access token from your Mastodon instance's developer settings. The token is stored in BFrost's worker settings — never committed to source.
author: @ccascio

versions.changelog: Ship full source-backed bundle with config surface, dashboard bundle, and current WorkerJobManifest scheduler contract.
Fix: truncation now respects Unicode code points, not byte length.
versions.yankReason: Superseded by source-backed bundle with current BFrost worker contract.

versions.changelog: Added hashtag injection from article tags. Configurable max-hashtags limit.
versions.yankReason: Superseded by source-backed bundle with current BFrost worker contract.

versions.changelog: Custom post templates via settings. URL append on truncation.
versions.yankReason: Superseded by source-backed bundle with current BFrost worker contract.

versions.changelog: Initial public release.
versions.yankReason: Superseded by source-backed bundle with current BFrost worker contract.

name: RSS Harvester
tagline: Polls RSS and Atom feeds on a schedule and publishes news.article items to the Item Bus.
description:
A producer worker that fetches one or more RSS/Atom feed URLs on a configurable schedule and pushes each new entry as a `news.article` item onto the BFrost Item Bus. Deduplication is keyed on the entry's `<link>` element — an article is only published once per source URL.

Feed URLs are declared in worker settings as a newline-separated list. Supports both RSS 2.0 and Atom 1.0.
author: @ccascio

versions.changelog: Ship full source-backed bundle with config surface, dashboard bundle, and current WorkerJobManifest scheduler contract.
Fix: handle feeds with missing pubDate by falling back to current time.
versions.yankReason: Superseded by source-backed bundle with current BFrost worker contract.

versions.changelog: Added Atom 1.0 support alongside RSS 2.0.
versions.yankReason: Superseded by source-backed bundle with current BFrost worker contract.

versions.changelog: Initial release. RSS 2.0 polling with deduplication by entry link.
versions.yankReason: Superseded by source-backed bundle with current BFrost worker contract.

name: WordPress Publisher
tagline: Publishes news.article items from the Item Bus to your WordPress site via the REST API.
description:
A consumer worker that subscribes to `news.article` items on the BFrost Item Bus and turns each one into a WordPress post. Supports configurable publish status (draft, publish, pending), custom post types, per-run deduplication, and a runtime dashboard.

The install bundle includes the worker manifest, TypeScript backend source, dashboard bundle source, and README.
author: @ccascio

versions.changelog: Ship full source-backed bundle with matching worker id, dashboard bundle, and current BFrost worker contract.
Added configurable publish status (draft, publish, pending) and custom post type support.
versions.yankReason: Superseded by source-backed bundle with current BFrost worker contract.

versions.changelog: Per-run deduplication: the same article is never posted twice.
versions.yankReason: Superseded by source-backed bundle with current BFrost worker contract.

versions.changelog: Initial release. Posts news.article items as WordPress posts via the REST API.
versions.yankReason: Superseded by source-backed bundle with current BFrost worker contract.

name: Anthropic Provider
tagline: Use your Anthropic account to power BFrost with Claude — no local GPU required.
description:
Built-in cloud LLM provider that routes all model calls to Anthropic's API using your API key. Supports the full Claude model family for chat, reasoning, and tool use.

This is a core infrastructure worker — it ships with every BFrost installation and cannot be removed.
author: @bfrost

versions.changelog: Ships with BFrost. No standalone install needed.

name: Article Reader
tagline: Lets the assistant open a web page and read it back in clean text — the same kind of reader-view your browser gives you.
description:
Built-in tool worker that fetches a URL, strips ads and navigation, and returns the main article content as clean text. Used internally by the news digest and research workers, and available to the assistant for on-demand reading.

This is a core infrastructure worker — it ships with every BFrost installation and cannot be removed.
author: @bfrost

versions.changelog: Ships with BFrost. No standalone install needed.

name: Assistant Memory
tagline: Lets the assistant remember things you tell it across conversations. Memories stay on your machine.
description:
Built-in tool worker that gives the BFrost assistant persistent memory. Tell it your preferences, recurring instructions, or facts to remember — they are stored locally and recalled automatically in future conversations.

This is a core infrastructure worker — it ships with every BFrost installation and cannot be removed.
author: @bfrost

versions.changelog: Ships with BFrost. No standalone install needed.

name: Bus & History Inspector
tagline: Lets the assistant look up what is in your queue, search past items, and summarise recent runs — just ask.
description:
Built-in tool worker that exposes Item Bus queries and run history to the BFrost assistant. Use natural language to ask what news articles are queued, look up recent research notes, or check how a job performed last week.

This is a core infrastructure worker — it ships with every BFrost installation and cannot be removed.
author: @bfrost

versions.changelog: Ships with BFrost. No standalone install needed.

name: Conversational Control Panel
tagline: Tell the assistant to enable a job, change its schedule, or trigger a run — without opening the dashboard.
description:
Built-in tool worker that gives the BFrost assistant a set of control functions: list jobs and their schedules, enable or disable any job, change a cron expression, or trigger an immediate run — all from a chat message.

This is a core infrastructure worker — it ships with every BFrost installation and cannot be removed.
author: @bfrost

versions.changelog: Ships with BFrost. No standalone install needed.

name: Daily News Digest
tagline: Pulls articles from your chosen sources, scores them for quality, and queues a short digest for review.
description:
A producer worker that searches Google Custom Search for articles matching your configured interests, scores them by source quality, deduplicates them, and queues them for review.

Optionally fetches article content for richer previews. Source quality rules (allow/block/prefer hosts and a minimum score) are configurable from the dashboard.

This is a built-in BFrost plugin worker. If you have deleted it, you can restore it here.
author: @bfrost

versions.changelog: Initial installable package for the Daily News Digest built-in worker.

name: Discord Channel
tagline: Have BFrost ping a Discord channel when a job runs, fails, or needs your attention.
description:
Built-in channel adapter that sends operator notifications to a Discord webhook. BFrost posts a message to the configured webhook URL whenever a scheduled job finishes, an error occurs, or an item needs approval.

This is a core infrastructure worker — it ships with every BFrost installation and cannot be removed.
author: @bfrost

versions.changelog: Ships with BFrost. No standalone install needed.

name: Google Web Search
tagline: Gives the assistant and other workers a way to look things up on the web using your Google Custom Search account.
description:
Built-in tool worker that wraps Google Custom Search API. The assistant can run web searches on demand; the news digest and research workers use it internally to gather sources.

Requires a Google Custom Search Engine ID and API key configured in worker settings.

This is a core infrastructure worker — it ships with every BFrost installation and cannot be removed.
author: @bfrost

versions.changelog: Ships with BFrost. No standalone install needed.

name: LM Studio Provider
tagline: Run AI models locally via LM Studio — no API keys, no monthly bill, your data stays on your machine.
description:
Built-in local LLM provider that connects BFrost to LM Studio's REST API. BFrost can start, stop, and load models through the LM Studio server so all inference stays on your hardware.

This is a core infrastructure worker — it ships with every BFrost installation and cannot be removed.
author: @bfrost

versions.changelog: Ships with BFrost. No standalone install needed.

name: OpenAI Provider
tagline: Use your OpenAI account to power BFrost with GPT models — no local GPU required.
description:
Built-in cloud LLM provider that routes all model calls to OpenAI's API using your API key. Supports GPT-4o, GPT-4, and other available models for chat and tool use.

This is a core infrastructure worker — it ships with every BFrost installation and cannot be removed.
author: @bfrost

versions.changelog: Ships with BFrost. No standalone install needed.

name: Post to X
tagline: Turns approved digest items into X posts. Drafts go out only after you say yes — nothing posted behind your back.
description:
A consumer worker that selects approved queue items, generates X posts with an LLM, and either posts them directly or stages them as drafts for review.

Configurable character limit, tone, template, and post timing. Requires X OAuth credentials.

This is a built-in BFrost plugin worker. If you have deleted it, you can restore it here.
author: @bfrost

versions.changelog: Initial installable package for the Post to X built-in worker.

name: Research Notes
tagline: Writes a Markdown research note for each topic on a schedule. Notes are saved locally to read, edit, and keep.
description:
A producer worker that searches Google Custom Search for your configured research topics, fetches article content, and uses an LLM to synthesize a durable Markdown research note saved to your local filesystem.

Configurable topics, result count, date restriction, and synthesis prompt. Notes are indexed in worker KV for easy browsing.

This is a built-in BFrost plugin worker. If you have deleted it, you can restore it here.
author: @bfrost

versions.changelog: Initial installable package for the Research Notes built-in worker.

name: Telegram Channel
tagline: Chat with your BFrost assistant from Telegram — text, voice notes, and photos all work.
description:
Built-in channel adapter that connects your BFrost assistant to a Telegram bot. Once your bot token is configured, BFrost listens for messages and replies inline. Voice notes are transcribed automatically when Whisper is available.

This is a core infrastructure worker — it ships with every BFrost installation and cannot be removed.
author: @bfrost

versions.changelog: Ships with BFrost. No standalone install needed.
